title: Broken Access Control (privilege escalation)
summary: A regular user reaches an admin-only user-management panel. Each stage adds a stronger but still-bypassable check, until deny-by-default server-side authorization on every admin route.
goal: You're logged in as alice (role user). Reach the admin-only panel at /admin — or promote yourself to admin — even though the UI hides it from regular users.
goalSecure: Try the earlier tricks — every admin route should deny a regular user (deny by default), while your normal user pages still work.
rootCause: Authorization for privileged functionality is missing, decided from client-controlled input, or applied inconsistently, so a regular user can reach admin-only endpoints simply by requesting them.
lessons: Enforce authorization on the server for every privileged endpoint — hiding the link in the UI is not access control.
Never decide a user's role from client-controlled input (cookies, headers, form fields); use the authenticated server-side session.
Guard the actions, not just the pages — state-changing endpoints (promote, delete) need the same check as the view.
Deny by default: protect the whole privileged area with one central check, not a per-path list you can forget to extend.
Authorize on the actual path the app serves — if a gateway checks one URL but the back-end resolves another (e.g. from X-Original-URL), they disagree and access control is bypassed.

stages.title: Trust a client role cookie
stages.defense: Checks a role value sent by the client.

stages.title: Guards the page, not the action
stages.defense: Admin page checks role; the action doesn't.

stages.title: Trusts an internal-only header
stages.defense: Admin limited to "internal" requests via X-Forwarded-For.

stages.title: Front-end path check, back-end URL override
Deny-by-default authorization
stages.defense: Session role enforced on every admin route.
